mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances when unemployment rises above the threshold, invalidating the labor demand channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP growth and stable consumer price inflation indicates robust economic demand without overheating, supporting higher corporate profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3% annualized while GDPC1 grows, resulting in a negative out-of-sample Sharpe ratio.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain efficiency, which immediately boosts market sentiment and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain flat or negative during periods of industrial production acceleration, with a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases household income and consumer expenditure, driving higher corporate revenues and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for payroll growth versus 20-day returns falls below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows, indicating no predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp spike in retail auto sales indicates strong consumer credit utilization and durable goods demand, which signals near-term earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS exceeds 1.5 standard deviations above its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in 70% of cases when auto sales breach the threshold, invalidating the demand transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in the federal funds rate and consumer price inflation compress real yields and raise discount rates, forcing equity valuations lower and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a positive out-of-sample return distribution with a p-value below 0.05, contradicting the valuation compression mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Three consecutive months of declining industrial production signal a contraction in manufacturing output and supply chain bottlenecks, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances following three months of industrial production declines, refuting the supply contraction premise.
